serviceNotes.engine oil: Polo AW follows the same VW Group fixed servicing logic as other EU VWs: oil and filter at each inspection service, typically 15,000 km or 12 months under the time-and-distance regime in the maintenance booklet.
serviceNotes.cabin filter: Cabin/pollen filter is scheduled on the manufacturer maintenance plan (often 30,000 km / 24 months on fixed-interval schedules).
serviceNotes.air filter: Engine air filter replacement is called for at the longer inspection interval in VW A0 maintenance documentation (commonly 60,000 km for petrol).
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Petrol engines list spark plug renewal in the official schedule; 60,000 km is representative for MPI/TSI four-cylinders—check your engine code.
serviceNotes.DSG gearbox fluid: DSG-equipped Polos benefit from fluid/filter service around 60,000 km as a longevity measure beyond the factory lifetime claim.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Coolant additive pack depletes over time; refresh around 120,000 km protects the cooling circuit and cabin heater core.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: VW specifies brake fluid replacement every 2 years on the fixed-interval EU schedule, approximately every 60,000 km.
serviceNotes.check brake pads: Brake pad inspection is part of the VW periodic maintenance programme.
